commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Add CCIS analysis results: correlations, XGBoost, SHAP, LOSO, cross-dataset

Key findings:
- 34,557 rows across LLFF/T&T, 14 methods, 4 budgets (k=10/15/20/25)
- XGBoost R²=0.61 (all metrics), Spearman rho=0.82
- CLIP distance is best single predictor (rho=0.22)
- FVS-Angular is best geometric metric (rho=0.18)
- SHAP: visual metrics (36%) > geometric (24.7%)
- Binary classification AUC=0.92 for PSNR>=25 dB
- LOSO: 0/14 scenes with R²>0 (LOSO failure persists)
- Cross-dataset: in-domain R²>0.93, cross-domain R²<0

commit.message:
6 new adaptive model candidates + 64-bit mask migration

Add IndirectByte, WordGap, RunLength, Nibble, ColumnPos, IndirectWord
as adaptive pruning candidates. With all 30 models active (no pruning),
compression improves on every tested file vs hand-tuned 7-model default:
E.coli -3.3%, web -1.5%, bible -0.3%, enwik8 +0.4%.

Infrastructure changes:
- ModelType enum: 27→33 values; all mask types uint→ulong (64-bit)
- Mixer.MaxInputs: 26→40; stackalloc buffers 27→41 in encode/decode
- Archive: NumInputs validation raised to 40; masks serialized as ulong
- New models are zero-cost when disabled (_newModelsActive gate)
- WordGapModel.cs: 4-word hash history for non-adjacent word context

commit.message:
Fix: redirect non-members from direct game URL to join page

When a user visits /game/[code] directly (e.g. via shared link in text/WhatsApp)
and they are not already a member of the game, redirect them to /join/[code].

/join/[code] has the full join flow: Join Game, Join Waitlist, Claim Spot,
Leave Game — identical to what entering the code on the home page shows.

This makes direct URL sharing work identically to the manual code-entry flow.
The redirect fires once after the initial data load using a ref guard to prevent
repeated redirects from the 5-second polling interval.

Groups already had this pattern (router.replace to /group/join/[code]) — this
applies the same fix to games.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: clean up PyInstaller build warnings in app.spec

- Remove pkg_resources.py2_warn hidden import (dropped in modern setuptools)
- Replace broad collect_submodules("google") with specific imports to avoid
  the grpc/operations_v1 warning from unused gRPC submodules
- Exclude pycparser generated files, charset_normalizer compiled extension,
  and google.api_core.operations_v1 (requires grpc, not used here)

Build now completes with zero warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate UI hitch on first sound effect playback (#181)

`new AudioContext()` takes ~500ms in Electron (OS audio subsystem init).
Move it to app startup so the cost is paid during load, not on the first
user interaction. The context starts suspended per autoplay policy and is
resumed on first play().

Also removes the warmup() indirection and nullable fields — the engine
is fully initialized in its constructor now.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add telemetry tracking and evolve auto-improve prompt

Add client-side interaction telemetry (button clicks, canvas taps,

reads telemetry data to inform its decisions. Updated prompt to allow
creative divergence and radical reimagination of the app over time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

CI: copy CEF ANGLE/SwiftShader dylibs next to vivid executable

On headless CI runners without Metal GPU, CEF falls back to software
ANGLE/SwiftShader but looks for libGLESv2.dylib and libEGL.dylib next
to the process executable rather than inside the framework bundle.
Copy them out of Chromium Embedded Framework.framework/Libraries/ to
make them discoverable.

commit.message:
feat(barry): add query expander skill and OR-logic search (Phase 1)

This implements Phase 1 of the Barry AI Transformation Plan - the macro fix for synonym problems.

Changes:
1. Added barry-query-expander.ts skill that uses Claude Haiku to expand user queries into technical manual terminology
2. Wired query expander into OpenClaw pipeline as Context Gatherer (runs after domain guard and knowledge lookup, before manual/RPS search)
3. Modified manual search to use OR logic instead of AND logic when expanded terms are available
4. Modified RPS search to use expanded terms in addition to hardcoded mappings
5. Follows Forever Architecture - no changes to core routing logic, only context gathering

Example: 'indicator stalk' → ['combination switch', 'Lenkstockschalter', 'turn signal lever', 'column switch'] → finds results in German technical manuals

Cost: ~/bin/zsh.002 per query (Claude Haiku, 200 tokens max)
Performance: +~300ms to pipeline (~3.7s → ~4.0s total)

commit.message:
Classify PowerOperatorInConstantExpr → §11.4.3 [skip ci]

The ** (power) operator is formally defined in §11.4.3 "Arithmetic operators", which contains Table 11-3 defining `a ** b` as "a to the power of b", Table 11-4 with integral power operator rules, and the normative prose specifying result types and x-result semantics. §11.2.1 only states that constant expressions may use any operator from Table 11-1 but does not define the power operator itself.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: update UI with improved styling and move API key to env variable

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: parallelize diagnostic report formatting with rayon

Report formatting was sequential, taking ~203ms (40% of total) when
formatting 1.2M diagnostics on the Grafana dataset. Parallelize with
rayon::par_iter to format each file concurrently, then print sequentially
to maintain deterministic output order.

Results on Grafana (6,192 files, 710+ rules):
- Report: 203ms → ~90ms (2.3x faster)
- Total: 505ms → ~400ms (20% overall improvement)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Replace all Breezy animations from updated sprite sheet

New 180x180 uniform frame size for all animations:
- Static dribble (6 frames), running dribble (8 frames)
- Jumpshot (7 frames, play once, hold last frame, ball hidden)
- Stepback (4 frames, play once, dead ball rules preserved)
- Crossover (4 frames, new dedicated animation)
- Defense backpedal (4 frames, loop while moving in defense)
- Defense shuffle/static (2 frames, frame 0 = static stance)
- Steal reach-in (3 frames, play once)

All animations use ANIM_SCALE=1.1, origin (0.5, 0.97).
All existing gameplay rules preserved (plant-then-jump, face hoop,
stepback dead ball, crossover defender shift, etc.)

commit.message:
Fix Avatar3D dispose race condition causing postProcessManager null error

Replace GlowLayer (which uses postProcessManager and crashes when the
scene disposes mid-shader-compile) with direct emissiveColor on meshes.
Stabilize useEffect dependencies so the scene only rebuilds when the
model or compact mode changes, not on every render. Add disposed guards
at every async boundary.

v1.0.19: rich card options + state-aware icons

New config options (all available in the visual editor):
- compact: tighter rows (44px height, smaller icon/font) for dashboard panels
- max_visible: cap how many suggestions show (0 = all)
- tap_action: "execute" (default) / "more-info" / "expand" (shows reason only)
- show_feedback: hide/show thumbs up/down buttons
- show_confidence_border: hide/show score left-border
- show_section_headers: hide/show "Suggested for You" / "Scenes" / "Worth Trying"

Icon improvements:
- State-aware icons: lights show outline when off, covers show open/closed,
  locks show locked/unlocked, media players show cast-connected when playing,
  fans show fan-off, vacuums show variant when not cleaning
- Fallback chain: HA entity icon → Ollama icon → state-aware → domain default
